query: What is Elon Musk's current net worth according to Bloomberg Billionaires Index in early July 2026? Has it recovered above $1 trillion after the late June 2026 tech selloff? Include recent Tesla and SpaceX share price and valuation trajectory.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Bottom line:** As of early July 2026, Elon Musk's Bloomberg Billionaires Index net worth is **~$932 billion** (Forbes: $916B) — **below $1 trillion**. He has **not** durably recovered above the trillion mark after the late-June tech selloff; his wealth keeps oscillating around the $1T line.

**The arc:**
- **June 12**: SpaceX IPO (SPCX) made him the world's first trillionaire (~$1.1T)
- **June 16**: Intraday peak of ~$1.45T as SpaceX hit ~$225
- **June 22–24**: SpaceX plunged ~31% + $116B of Tesla shares restricted → dropped to $946B (Bloomberg)
- **July 1**: Brief rebound reversed; Forbes had him at $994.1B, again sub-$1T
- **Early-mid July**: Official indices ~$916–932B

**Tesla (TSLA):** $406.55 (July 9), ~$1.53T market cap, ~18.5% below 52-week high; supported by Miami robotaxi launch and strong European sales.

**SpaceX (SPCX):** $152.16 (July 9), ~$2.0T market cap, ~32.6% below its June 16 peak and near post-IPO lows. IPO priced at $135 (~$1.75T target). Analysts are sharply divided — Bloomberg cited a $2.2T valuation test, while independent analysis pegs fair value nearer $1.25T.

**Important caveat:** Sources diverge — official Bloomberg/Forbes closing figures show sub-$1T, while some third-party live trackers show ~$1.07–1.11T. Whether he's "above $1 trillion" depends on the measure (index close vs. live tracker vs. intraday) and SpaceX's extreme volatility swings his net worth by tens of billions daily.

key_facts: Elon Musk's net worth per Bloomberg Billionaires Index is ~$932 billion as of July 2026 (Forbes: $916 billion) — BELOW the $1 trillion threshold.
He has NOT durably recovered above $1 trillion after the late-June 2026 tech selloff; net worth has 'frequently dropped to under $1 trillion since late June.'
Musk became the world's first trillionaire on June 12, 2026 when SpaceX IPO'd (ticker SPCX).
His net worth peaked at an all-time intraday high of ~$1.45 trillion the week after the IPO (around June 16, 2026).
A SpaceX selloff (~31% from peak) plus restrictions on ~$116B of Musk's Tesla shares dropped him below $1T by June 24, 2026 (Bloomberg: $946B).
On July 1, 2026, Forbes reported SpaceX fell 6.2%, cutting his net worth by $59B to $994.1 billion — again below trillionaire status.
Tesla (TSLA) closed at $406.55 on July 9, 2026 (+3.17%), market cap ~$1.53 trillion, ~18.5% below its 52-week high of $498.83.
SpaceX (SPCX) closed at $152.16 on July 9, 2026, market cap ~$2.0 trillion, ~32.6% below its June 16 peak of ~$225.64 and near its post-IPO low.
SpaceX IPO priced at $135/share (~$1.75T target valuation), raised ~$5B (largest IPO in history), debut close $160.95 (+19%).
Some third-party live net worth trackers show ~$1.07-1.11T (July 9), running above the official Bloomberg/Forbes closing figures.
Musk's stakes: ~42% of SpaceX and ~20% of Tesla; wealth managed by Excession LLC family office.
